release: Wardex 0.3.0-alpha2

Tab-completion hardening.

- Fix `~/Downloads/x<TAB>` producing garbled output — add
  `ValueHint::FilePath`/`AnyPath` to `ctf import`, `--config`, `search`,
  and `info` so clap_complete routes through its tilde-aware path
  completer (custom.rs:298–307).
- Migrate Home Manager module off deprecated `programs.zsh.initExtra`
  to `programs.zsh.initContent` with `lib.mkAfter`. Leaves
  `programs.bash.initExtra` alone (not deprecated).
- Rewrite CTF completion helpers to be instruction-based:
  - `resolve_ctf_root` now uses `Config::load()` instead of a hand-rolled
    YAML scan, honoring `paths.ctf_root` exactly as the main binary.
  - `challenge_completer` no longer silently picks the lexically-largest
    event directory as "latest" — returns empty, user must run
    `wardex ctf use <event>`.
  - `category_completer` reads `config.ctf.default_categories` instead
    of a hard-coded six-category list.
  - `WX_PATHS_CTF_ROOT` now tilde-expands.
- Fix case-sensitivity inconsistency in `category_completer` — now
  matches prefixes case-insensitively like the other two completers.

Tests: 63 passing (12 unit + 51 integration). Rewrote the completion
test block with an `isolate_env()` helper that redirects
`XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, `WARDEX_STATE_FILE`, and clears `WX_*` overrides so
tests exercise the real config loader with a tempdir.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

git add src/main.rs src/engine/ctf/completions.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: Handle bare `~<TAB>` to prevent zsh user-name fallback

`wardex ctf import ~<TAB>` was listing every account in /etc/passwd
(nixbld1..32, systemd-*, sddm, …) — zsh's POSIX `~user/` home-dir
completion firing as a fallback because clap_complete returned no
candidates for bare `~`.

Root cause: clap_complete 4.6's path completer (custom.rs:298) only
recognises `~` when it's the *parent* component of the typed word,
i.e. `~/foo` works but bare `~` returns empty.

Fix: add a wardex-owned `complete_path` function, exposed as
`any_path_completer` (for `search`, `info`) and `file_path_completer`
(for `ctf import`, `--config`). Attached via `ArgValueCompleter` on
those args. `value_hint` is kept so AOT completion scripts
(`wardex completions zsh`) remain correct.

Behaviour:
- `~`           → suggest `~/` (one candidate)
- `~/`, `~/x`   → list $HOME entries with `~/` prefix preserved
- `/abs/…`      → list under absolute path
- `rel/…`, `x`  → list under cwd

Covered by three new unit tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git add flake.nix flake.lock .gitignore && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
build(nix): Migrate to crane for incremental Rust builds

`rustPlatform.buildRustPackage` rebuilt all ~200 dependency crates
whenever the wardex source changed, making `nh os switch` with wardex
as a flake input painfully slow.

Migrate to `crane` (ipetkov), which splits the build into two
derivations:

- `cargoArtifacts`: builds the dep graph. Keyed by Cargo.lock +
  toolchain hash; source-only changes to wardex do not invalidate it.
- `wardex` package: builds our code on top of the cached deps.

Downstream flake consumers (nh os switch, CI) rebuild only wardex
itself on each bump instead of the full dep tree — ~80–90% faster on
typical source changes.

Also:

- Source is filtered via `lib.cleanSourceWith` wrapping
  `craneLib.filterCargoSources`, plus `README.md` (needed because
  `src/lib.rs` uses `include_str!("../README.md")` as crate-level
  rustdoc).
- `nativeBuildInputs` no longer carries `rustToolchain` directly;
  crane injects the toolchain via `overrideToolchain`.
- `checks.wardex` added so `nix flake check` exercises the package
  against the shared dep cache.
- `result`, `result-*` added to .gitignore (nix build symlinks).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

See CHANGELOG.md for user-facing notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

git add src/engine/ctf/completions.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test: Make tilde-completion test hermetic for Nix sandbox

`path_completer_tilde_slash_lists_home_entries` asserted
`!results.is_empty()` on $HOME, which broke `nix build` — inside the
Nix sandbox $HOME is a minimal tempdir with no entries, so the test
panicked and failed `nh os switch`.

Stub $HOME to a controlled tempdir populated with a known file + dir,
assert the specific expected candidates (`~/Downloads/`, `~/notes.txt`)
instead of "any entries". Restore $HOME at the end and mark the test
`#[serial_test::serial]` since it mutates a process-global env var.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

git add tests/cli_integration.rs && git diff --cached --stat && echo "---" && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test: Replace deprecated Command::cargo_bin with cargo_bin_cmd! macro

assert_cmd 2.1.0 deprecated `Command::cargo_bin(name)` because its
`current_exe()`-based target-dir discovery breaks under custom cargo
build-dirs (CARGO_TARGET_DIR, workspace-level `[build] target-dir`,
and some crane stagings).

Replace all 14 call sites with the `cargo_bin_cmd!()` macro, which
resolves the binary via `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_wardex")` — a compile-
time env var cargo emits for integration-test harnesses. Correct
regardless of build-dir layout.

All 66 tests pass, clippy is now warning-free (was generating 14
deprecation warnings per `cargo test` run, including in the Nix build
check phase).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
